title: 10 best herbal essential oils for sunburn
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for sunburn are plant-derived oils that help alleviate sunburn symptoms by promoting skin healing, reducing inflammation, and soothing pain. The best herbal essential oils for sunburn include Calendula officinalis, Arnica montana, Aloe barbadensis, Hypericum perforatum, Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe vera, Hamamelis virginiana, Echinacea purpurea, Symphytum officinale, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, sunburn can cause prolonged discomfort, blistering, and even long-term skin damage, leading to premature aging, scarring, and increased risk of skin cancer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with sunburn because they contain soothing and calming properties that quickly reduce redness and inflammation. The aloe vera gel in these oils has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to cool and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of aloe essential oils help to hydrate the skin, promoting healing and preventing peeling. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from sunburn and promote faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn by reducing inflammation in the skin.
Sothothic and moisturizing: The gel-like substance in aloe vera contains vitamins A, C, and E, which help to soothe and hydrate the skin, promoting faster healing and reducing peeling.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, preventing further damage to skin cells and accelerating the recovery process.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them ideal for soothing sunburned skin.
Rhyzomes: Contains gel-like substances that help calm and cool sunburned areas, reducing redness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and slice them into thin pieces.
Combine sliced aloe vera with distilled water in a ratio of 1:4 and allow mixture to steep for 24 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids and achieve clear liquid.
Add 10% tea tree essential oil and 5% lavender essential oil to the filtered liquid and stir well.
Allow the mixture to sit for an additional 2 weeks before decanting into glass bottles and storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with sunburn because it contains a wealth of soothing and calming properties. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds work to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburned skin. Additionally, its antioxidant-rich composition helps protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation. When applied topically, pot marigold essential oils can help ease pain, promote healing, and leave skin feeling soft, smooth, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: This property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.
Antioxidant property: As an antioxidant, Pot Marigold Essential Oil neutralizes free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can further damage skin cells and exacerbate sunburn.

Wound healing property: This property promotes tissue repair, regeneration, and hydration, helping to accelerate the recovery process and reduce the risk of infection or scarring associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of calcium and silica, which can help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Buds: Pot marigold buds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak blooming stage, selecting only those with vibrant yellow petals and no signs of damage.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers using air circulation or desiccant methods for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried calendula flowers in a still with 30% water at atmospheric pressure for 4-6 hours, collecting the essential oil as it separates.
Filter the resulting essential oil through coffee filter paper or a centrifuge to remove impurities and sediment, then transfer to dark glass bottles for storage.
Allow the calendula essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before use, shaking the bottle daily to facilitate blending of its constituent components.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with sunburn because of its soothing and calming properties. When applied topically to affected areas, it can help reduce redness and inflammation, promoting relaxation and ease. The oil's antiseptic and antibacterial properties also work to prevent infection and speed up the healing process. Additionally, lavender's cooling and moisturizing effects can provide relief from burning sensations and dry skin, leaving skin feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender essential oil reduces inflammation caused by sunburn, helping to ease pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant: English Lavender essential oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation.
Soothe and Calm: English Lavender essential oil has a calming and soothing effect on the skin, helping to ease itching, burning, and discomfort associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Flowers: Contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps protect skin from further damage and promotes collagen production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in abundance weighing approximately three hundred grams.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them thinly on paper bags for seven days to reduce moisture levels.
Place one part dried lavandula angustifolia flowers into a large glass jar and add five parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Close the jar tightly and leave it in a cool dark place for four weeks to allow for gentle cold pressing extraction of essential oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container and discard solids after three hours to yield approximately one hundred milliliters of essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with sunburn because of its cooling and soothing properties. When applied topically, peppermint oil can help reduce heat and inflammation caused by sun exposure. Its anti-inflammatory compounds work to calm redness and itching, while also helping to ease discomfort and promote relaxation. Additionally, peppermint's refreshing aroma can even help to revitalize and rejuvenate the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburned skin.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of peppermint essential oil can provide relief from the stinging, burning, and itching sensations often experienced after sun exposure.
Cooling and soothing: Peppermint essential oil has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help reduce heat, itchiness, and discomfort caused by sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain high amounts of menthol, which provides cooling relief and reduces inflammation, making them ideal for soothing sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are rich in antioxidants and menthol, which help to calm redness, itching, and swelling caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and dry them using a clean air circulation drying rack for two weeks.
Use a food processor to finely chop 250g of dried peppermint leaves and store them in an airtight container.
Steam distill the chopped peppermint leaves using 500ml of water at 100°c for one hour, collecting the resulting essential oil in a glass bottle.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities or sediment, then store it in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid.
Dilute the peppermint essential oil with 10ml of sweet almond oil and apply topically as needed for sunburn relief, ideally within an hour of exposure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When applied topically to affected areas, it can soothe and calm red, irritated skin, reducing heat and discomfort. Its natural antibacterial qualities also help prevent infection and promote wound healing. Additionally, tea tree oil's antioxidant properties work to neutralize free radicals caused by UV damage, further aiding in the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Cooling and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Chamomile tea contains apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation. This can help ease the discomfort and redness associated with sunburn.

Antioxidant and Free Radical Scavenging Abilities: Green Tea contains high levels of antioxidants like catechins, which help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation and free radicals. This may help prevent premature aging and promote faster healing.

Moisturizing and Soothing Properties: Aloe Vera tea is known for its hydrating properties, which can help reduce peeling and dryness associated with sunburn. The gel of the aloe vera plant also contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in bioactive compounds, tea tree leaves contain potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant contain high levels of sesquiterpenes, which provide a natural antiseptic and analgesic effect to alleviate sunburn pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at dawn when the dew is still present for optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using a dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or until crispy.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a copper alembic still with 10 parts water to 1 part leaf material for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth into dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.
Use the melaleuca alternifolia essential oil by adding 2-3 drops to a carrier oil or lotion and applying topically to sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, soothing the skin. The oil's natural cooling effect also provides instant relief from burning sensation, while its antioxidant compounds help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV rays. Additionally, ginger's antimicrobial properties prevent infection and promote faster healing, making it an effective and natural remedy for sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger oil helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing redness and swelling. This property is due to the presence of gingerols and shogaols, which inhibit prostaglandins and cytokines responsible for inflammatory responses.

Antioxidant: Herbal ginger essential oils rich in antioxidants help neutralize free radicals generated from UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting collagen synthesis, which aids in skin regeneration and improved appearance.
Analgesic: Ginger oil's analgesic properties relieve pain associated with sunburn, reducing discomfort and itchiness caused by heat, inflammation, and damaged skin cells.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the ginger plant, which contains high levels of compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Roots: Ginger roots contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of 3-4 inches long from a healthy plant for extraction.
Dry the ginger roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or until brittle.
Grind 100g of dried ginger into fine powder using a spice grinder and store in an airtight container.
Mix 1 part ginger powder with 5 parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids and discard the solids, resulting in approximately 30ml of ginger essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with sunburn because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that work wonders in soothing and calming the skin. Its natural astringent qualities help to reduce redness and swelling, while its cooling effect provides instant relief from discomfort and heat. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils can provide effective relief from sunburn, promoting a speedy recovery and reducing the risk of further irritation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil contains powerful antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and worsen sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, helping to soothe and calm the affected area.

Wound-healing properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil contains bioactive compounds that promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, Tasmanian Blue Gum roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation, pain, and itching associated with sunburn, making it an effective natural remedy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning for optimal potency and aroma quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or cloth sheets for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using 0.5 liters of distilled water and 1% steam pressure at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with sunburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin. When applied topically, chamomile oil can reduce redness and swelling caused by sun exposure, providing instant relief from discomfort and itching. Its gentle and non-irritating nature makes it suitable for all skin types, including sensitive skin. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can cause further damage to the skin, promoting a faster recovery from sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburn by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds in the skin.
Antioxidant property: Chamomile essential oil's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and lead to sunburn.
Soothing property: Chamomile essential oil calms and cools the skin, reducing discomfort and pain associated with sunburn, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and soothing compounds, chamomile roots are used to create a calming essential oil that helps reduce inflammation and itching caused by sunburn.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound, chamomile flowers are used to create an essential oil that soothes redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven at 60°c for two hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers using 100 grams of plant material and 500 ml water for three hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final matricaria chamomilla essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling caused by UV exposure. Its citrusy scent also provides a refreshing and calming relief to soothe itchy skin. Additionally, lemongrass oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from further damage and promote collagen production, speeding up the healing process and leaving skin looking smoother and more radiant. Its natural cooling effect also helps reduce discomfort and itching associated with sunburn.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass essential oil helps to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and geraniol, can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburned skin.

Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria, which can help prevent infection and promote wound healing in sunburned areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing sunburned skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain natural analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of lemongrass, rinsing them gently with cold water to remove impurities and debris.
Dry the lemongrass leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content.
Use a steam distiller to extract essential oil from dried lemongrass leaves, adding 10-12 grams of leaves per 100 milliliters of water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol using a separating funnel, allowing the oil to settle at the bottom for 30 minutes.
Bottle the extracted cymbopogon citratus essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids, storing them in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburned skin. The oil also has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing. Additionally, geranium essential oil has a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from discomfort and itching caused by sunburn.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Geranium essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as geraniol and linalool, help reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort caused by sunburn.
Antibacterial: Geranium essential oil's antimicrobial properties prevent bacterial infections that can occur when the skin is compromised due to sunburn, promoting a healthy recovery process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain soothing compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing, making them effective in alleviating sunburn discomfort.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which provide potent anti-inflammatory properties and protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems from the morning after dew has fallen.
Clean and dry the harvested material by gently shaking off excess moisture and laying it flat on paper towels for two hours.
Steam distill the dried pelargonium graveolens material using 200 ml of distilled water at a temperature range of 100-105°c for one hour.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the steam distillation mixture, filtering it through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle containing 5 ml of high-quality jojoba oil as a carrier.
Store the herbal pelargonium graveolens essential oil in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature, allowing it to mature for three days before use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sunburn with herbal essential oils is to dilute them in a carrier oil and apply topically. Geranium essential oil soothes and calms the skin, while peppermint oil cools and reduces inflammation. Lavender oil promotes relaxation and helps reduce itching and discomfort. Apply the blend to affected areas 2-3 times daily, massaging gently into the skin until fully absorbed. This treatment can provide relief from sunburn symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal essential oils are heat rash, minor burns, and scalds. Chamomile essential oil is often used to soothe and calm irritated skin, while peppermint oil can help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. Geranium oil has antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating minor cuts and scrapes. Additionally, lavender oil can promote healing and reduce the appearance of scars.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's ability to soothe and calm the skin helps to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn. Its antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, promoting skin repair and regeneration. Additionally, calendula oil's moisturizing and emollient properties help to hydrate and protect the skin, facilitating the healing process and reducing the risk of further damage.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: This phenolic compound helps with sunburn by exhibiting potent antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by UV radiation.

Neryl Acetate: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm and protect the skin from further damage after a sunburn, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain, swelling, and redness associated with sunburn, promoting comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are the most used part to make essential oils for sunburn, as they contain the highest concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis are also used due to their high content of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and protect the skin from sun damage.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis contain a significant amount of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in the healing of sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature calendula flowers on a dry day in the morning or late afternoon to minimize moisture content.
Dry the flowers in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture levels.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to distill the dried flowers with a ratio of 1:4 flowers to water for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water by decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove any impurities and store in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana essential oils helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and regenerative properties. It soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburn. The oil's antioxidants also help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, promoting the skin's natural healing process. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin repair, Arnica montana essential oils can help to alleviate the discomfort and pain of sunburn, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of further damage to the skin.
remedy_constituents: Tremetol (also known as Sesquiterpene lactones): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting skin healing and reducing the risk of sunburn.
Sesquiterpenes (including Thymol and β-Caryophyllene): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils for sunburn due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to create essential oils with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to reduce redness and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Buds: The buds contain volatile oils that help to soothe and calm sunburned skin, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers in late summer when they are in full bloom for 2-3 hours.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to prevent mold.
Use a commercial steam distiller to extract the essential oil from dried arnica montana flowers at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities for 30 minutes.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for 6 months.

The oil's cooling effect also helps to ease pain and itching caused by sunburn. By incorporating Aloe barbadensis essential oils into a sunburn treatment, you can help your skin recover faster and feel more comfortable. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps with sunburn by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Aloe-emodin: Contributes to reducing sunburn by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by UV radiation.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils for sunburn due to their high gel content and anti-inflammatory properties.
Gel inside the leaves: The gel is extracted from the leaves and used to make essential oils for sunburn due to its soothing and moisturizing properties.
Sap inside the leaves: The sap is extracted from the leaves and used to make essential oils for sunburn due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: They are used to make essential oils for sunburn due to their high gel content and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The gel is extracted from the leaves and used to make essential oils for sunburn due to its soothing and moisturizing properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe barbadensis leaves when they are at their darkest green color for optimal oil production.
Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly with a clean towel to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Use a juice extractor to extract the gel from the leaves and collect 500ml of pure gel.
Steam distill the aloe gel at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil from the gel.
Cool and filter the essential oil to separate it from any water or impurities present in the oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to soothe and calm sun-damaged skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The oil's antiseptic properties also help to prevent infection and promote skin healing, making it a natural remedy to alleviate sunburn symptoms and promote a speedy recovery. Its cooling effect provides relief from heat and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid helps with sunburn by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, which in turn accelerates skin healing.
Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe sunburned skin, reducing redness and promoting faster recovery.

Kaempferol: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting healing and reducing the risk of skin irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing sunburned skin.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce sunburn pain and inflammation.
Buds: Similar to flowers, buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide similar benefits for sunburn treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers on a dry sunny morning between june and august.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at a temperature range of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and a maximum capacity of 100ml.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil, commonly known as tea tree oil, contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn. Its cooling and moisturizing effects help to hydrate the skin, promoting a faster healing process and reducing the risk of infection, thereby providing relief from sunburn. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate sunburn symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cymene: This terpene constituent helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with sunburn.
Limonene: This terpene constituent has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation.
Linalool: This monoterpenoid constituent has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to calm and heal sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Melaleuca alternifolia to make essential oils for sunburn relief due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to make essential oils, which help to soothe and calm sunburned skin due to their antimicrobial properties.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (or underground stems) of Melaleuca alternifolia are sometimes used to make essential oils, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in sunburn recovery.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The oil's cooling effect helps to lower the skin's temperature, providing relief from the painful and itchy sensations associated with sunburn. Additionally, aloe vera's hydrating properties help to lock in moisture and promote skin regeneration, accelerating the healing process and minimizing the risk of scarring and peeling. This makes it a popular remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help calm and cool the skin, providing relief from sunburn discomfort.
Sitosterol: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the skin caused by sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Aloe vera plant contain the gel that is used to make essential oils for sunburn due to its soothing and cooling properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is also used to create essential oils that aid in moisturizing and hydrating sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Extract 4-6 ounces of aloe vera gel from the inner part of the leaf using a sharp knife.
Combine the extracted gel with 8 ounces of water in a blender and blend until smooth.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining pulp.
Transfer the strained mixture to a clean glass container and allow it to sit in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to ferment.
Distill the fermented mixture using a steam distillation apparatus to produce the aloe vera essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana essential oils helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It contains tannins, which have a cooling effect on the skin and help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn. The oil also has a soothing effect, calming irritated skin and promoting healing. Additionally, its astringent properties help to reduce fluid buildup and ease the discomfort associated with sunburn. This makes Hamamelis virginiana essential oil a natural and effective remedy for soothing sunburned skin.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelin: It helps with sunburn by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Tannins: Tannins help with sunburn by providing a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing moisture loss, and preventing further irritation.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin, a type of flavonoid, helps with sunburn by its potent antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and promote skin repair and regeneration.

Buds: The buds are used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs for steam distillation.
Steam distill the plant material at 100°c for 2 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2μm filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to sun damage. Additionally, Echinacea's ability to enhance skin's natural barrier function helps to lock in moisture and reduce redness. This combination of properties makes Echinacea purpurea essential oil an effective remedy for alleviating sunburn discomfort, promoting skin healing, and reducing the appearance of sunburned skin. It also aids in promoting overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Caffeic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Cichoric acid: This compound has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation and reduce inflammation.
Chlorogenic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting faster healing of sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect sunburned skin.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to the development of sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature echinacea purpurea flowers at peak bloom for optimal oil content and fragrance.
Dry echinacea flowers in a single layer at 40°c for 24 hours or until completely dry.
Steam distill 250 grams of dried echinacea flowers using 500 milliliters of water for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any remaining plant debris and particles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale essential oils helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil, extracted from the comfrey plant, contains allantoin, a compound known to promote skin healing and reduce redness. It also has antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from further damage and promote collagen production, reducing the appearance of sunburn blisters and promoting a smoother, more even skin tone. This makes Symphytum officinale essential oil a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Allantoin helps with sunburn by promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting the skin from further damage.
Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid helps with sunburn by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Nascent oxygen (O2): Nascent oxygen helps with sunburn by promoting tissue repair, reducing oxidative stress, and eliminating free radicals that cause damage to the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Symphytum officinale are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for sunburn because of their high concentration of allantoin, which soothes and calms skin irritations.
Flowers: Symphytum officinale flowers are also used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale contain allantoin and other compounds that promote skin healing and regeneration, making them useful in treating sunburn and other skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers at dawn to capture optimal potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100°c for 3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water through decantation within 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil using activated charcoal to remove impurities for 1 hour.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for long-term preservation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains flavonoids and terpenoids that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. It also helps to promote collagen production, which can aid in the healing process and reduce the appearance of sunburned skin. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba essential oil has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease the pain associated with sunburn.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Flavonoids (specifically quercetin and kaempferol): They have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by UV radiation and reduce oxidative stress.
Ginkgolides: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing after sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Buds: Contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing.
Seeds: Rich in ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate sunburn discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves at the right time when they are fully mature and dry.
Dry the leaves thoroughly for 2 weeks in a warm and well-ventilated area.
Steam distill 500g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for 3 hours to obtain the oil.
Filter the obtained oil to remove any impurities and sediment immediately after distillation.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.
